_comment: Agent-1 same-item gate calibration. CLIP is the gate; DINOv2 is reported-only evidence (measured to add no discrimination on this task — see live-proof-matcher memory / eval). All values env-overridable via SAME_ITEM_THRESHOLD / SAME_ITEM_GATE_SIGNAL.
_threshold_rationale: The deepfashion-inshop eval yields 0.896 for a strict ~5% false-accept on its SEMANTIC negatives, but its positives are front/side/back poses — HARDER than a real Meesho catalog-vs-live pair — so 0.896 over-rejects genuine items (the real same-kurti fixture scores clip/max 0.81 and would fail). We ship a recall-leaning 0.75, which passes the genuine fixture (0.81) and rejects the different-dress fixture (0.71), because the product requirement is 'only fail if it is a DIFFERENT product' and the challenge code (single-use) + reuse/liveness + human review backstop fraud. BELOW this bar an in-band VLM feature-comparison tie-breaker (main.py: SAME_ITEM_VLM_LO/HI; compares pattern/colour/print/logo/style, ignores background/lighting/angle) can still RESCUE a genuine seller — recall-only, it never vetoes a pass. MUST be re-validated on a real Meesho catalog-vs-live set; set SAME_ITEM_THRESHOLD=0.896 for the strict eval operating point.

calibration.note: No embedding separates same-category+same-colour look-alikes (all AUC 0.53-0.67); those are deferred to the challenge code, liveness, reuse detection, and human review. The visual gate's calibrated job is to catch obviously-different products (semantic).
